questions.text: Why does the Surah swear by morning brightness and the still night?
questions.answer: To teach that changes (like day and night) are normal, including pauses, and Allah’s care continues.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that delays in relief mean Allah hates a believer.
questions.answer: False. It shows Allah may test with pauses, but His care remains.

questions.text: How does Surah Ad-Duha correct despair during spiritual dryness?
questions.answer: It reassures that Allah has not left the servant and that future good is coming by Allah’s mercy.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Hereafter compared to dunya for the Prophet?
questions.answer: The Hereafter is better and more lasting, so believers should keep their priorities aligned.

questions.text: What is one lesson from the promise ‘your Lord will give you and you will be satisfied’?
questions.answer: Allah’s gifts are purposeful and complete; trust Allah’s timing and outcome.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach gratitude through recalling past blessings?
questions.answer: It reminds how Allah helped in vulnerability (orphanhood, searching, need) to strengthen trust now.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that remembering past hardships is only painful and should be avoided.
questions.answer: False. Remembering Allah’s past help strengthens faith and resilience.

questions.text: What does the command about the orphan teach about Islamic character?
questions.answer: Mercy and dignity for the vulnerable are central; do not oppress or humiliate them.

questions.text: What does the command about the petitioner teach about community ethics?
questions.answer: Treat people who ask with kindness and respect, even if you cannot give.

questions.text: What is a practical way to apply ‘do not repel the one who asks’ today?
questions.answer: Respond politely, offer what you can, or guide them to help—without harshness or shame.

questions.text: How can a believer ‘proclaim the favor of your Lord’ without showing off?
questions.answer: Speak with gratitude, attribute blessings to Allah, and use blessings in obedience and service.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that gratitude is only words and not action.
questions.answer: False. Gratitude includes how you treat others and how you use blessings.

questions.text: How does this Surah teach that Allah’s future support is built on past support?
questions.answer: It points to Allah’s care in earlier weakness as proof Allah will continue helping.

questions.text: What is one way this Surah can help someone dealing with loneliness?
questions.answer: It reminds that Allah did not abandon the Prophet, and Allah’s care is near for the believer too.

questions.text: How does Surah Ad-Duha connect spirituality with social mercy?
questions.answer: Comfort from Allah is followed by commands to be gentle with orphans and those who ask.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests a believer can be close to Allah while being harsh to the weak.
questions.answer: False. It ties closeness to mercy and gratitude.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about interpreting tests and pauses correctly?
questions.answer: Do not assume abandonment; keep hope and continue obedience until relief comes.

questions.text: How can a believer respond when they feel their dua is delayed?
questions.answer: Stay patient, continue worship, and remember Allah’s past favors and wisdom in timing.

questions.text: What is one daily practice inspired by this Surah’s tone of comfort?
questions.answer: Start the day with gratitude and end it by recalling one past mercy Allah granted you.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that the Prophet’s value depends on people’s opinions.
questions.answer: False. It centers Allah’s care and honor, not people’s rumors.

questions.text: What does 'Ad-Duha' refer to?
questions.answer: The morning brightness or the forenoon.

questions.text: What is the second oath in the Surah?
questions.answer: The night when it covers with darkness/is still.

questions.text: What was the rumor the disbelievers spread that this Surah refutes?
questions.answer: They said that Allah had forsaken Prophet Muhammad (SAW) because revelation had paused.

questions.text: What is the reassurance given in this Surah?
questions.answer: Your Lord has not taken leave of you, nor has He detested you.

questions.text: How does the Hereafter compare to the first [life] for the Prophet (SAW)?
questions.answer: The Hereafter is better for him than the first (worldly) life.

questions.text: What is the promise in this Surah about Allah’s gift and satisfaction?
questions.answer: Your Lord is going to give to you, and you will be satisfied.

questions.text: What three states of the Prophet's early life are mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: He was an orphan, he was wandering/lost (seeking truth), and he was poor/in need.

questions.text: How did Allah resolve the Prophet's state as an orphan?
questions.answer: He found him an orphan and gave him shelter.

questions.text: How did Allah resolve the Prophet's state of 'wandering'?
questions.answer: He found him lost/wandering and guided him.

questions.text: How did Allah resolve the Prophet's state of 'need'?
questions.answer: He found him in need and made him self-sufficient/wealthy.

questions.text: What is the command regarding the orphan in this Surah?
questions.answer: Do not oppress or treat the orphan with harshness.

questions.text: What is the command regarding the petitioner/beggar in this Surah?
questions.answer: Do not repel or scold the one who asks.

questions.text: What should the Prophet (and believers) do regarding the favor of their Lord?
questions.answer: Proclaim it / speak of it with gratitude.

questions.text: What is the 'favor' (Ni'mah) referred to at the end?
questions.answer: It includes the Quran, Prophethood, and all spiritual and material blessings.

questions.text: What is the emotional tone of Surah Ad-Duha?
questions.answer: One of intense comfort, affection, and reassurance.

questions.text: True or False: This Surah teaches that past help from Allah is a proof of future support.
questions.answer: True.

questions.text: Why is 'the night when it is still' mentioned after 'the morning brightness'?
questions.answer: It may represent the pause in revelation, which is as natural and necessary as the night following the day.

questions.text: What is the relationship between this Surah’s promise of satisfaction and the concept of intercession (shafa'ah)?
questions.answer: Scholars say the Prophet (SAW) will not be satisfied until his entire Ummah is forgiven.

questions.text: What does 'Fa-Aghna' (made self-sufficient) mean for the Prophet?
questions.answer: Contentment of the heart and the wealth Khadijah (RA) brought to him.

questions.text: How does this Surah instruct us to handle our past?
questions.answer: By reflecting on how Allah took care of us when we were vulnerable, to strengthen our current faith.
